description: External style-guide snippets, transcribed for close-paraphrase detection (human-eval build plan Session 35). Each entry captures a short rule or principle from a public source. The close-paraphrase tool (`tools/check_close_paraphrase.py`) compares ContentRX standards against these snippets and warns when a standard's rule text is suspiciously close to one without naming the source.
note: This corpus is intentionally small at seed time. Grow it as Robo audits new standards or adopts new sources. Each entry is a short transcription, fair-use quantity; attribution lives in the `source` field and (where available) the `url`.

snippets.text: Start with a verb that describes the specific action.
Use OK and Cancel as confirmation-dialog button labels.
Write in plain English. Use short words. Avoid jargon and acronyms.
Write in plain language so anyone can understand. This is not dumbing down; it is writing for everyone.
Prefer verbs that name the specific action over generic affirmatives like OK, especially on destructive confirmations.
Exclamation points should be earned in celebration, not piled on in errors. Too much excitement undermines a calm voice.
